edges.description: Occlusion creates a severely hypoperfused core and a potentially salvageable penumbra with residual perfusion.
Energy failure disrupts ionic gradients and glutamate handling, triggering calcium overload, oxidative injury, and cell death.
Ischemic injury activates vascular, glial, and immune responses in the neurovascular unit.

edges.source: Anticoagulation for atrial fibrillation-associated stroke prevention
Primary prevention through vascular risk-factor control

nodes.description: Anticoagulation, particularly direct oral anticoagulant therapy when appropriate, is used after ischemic stroke associated with atrial fibrillation to reduce recurrent embolic events while managing hemorrhage risk.
nodes.id: Anticoagulation for atrial fibrillation-associated stroke prevention

evidence.reference_title: Antithrombotic treatment for secondary prevention of stroke and other thromboembolic events in patients with stroke or transient ischemic attack and non-valvular atrial fibrillation: A European Stroke Organisation guideline.
evidence.snippet: In patients with atrial fibrillation and previous stroke or transient ischemic attack, oral anticoagulants reduce the risk of recurrence over antiplatelets or no antithrombotic treatment.

nodes.description: Antiplatelet therapy reduces recurrent ischemic events after non-cardioembolic ischemic stroke or high-risk transient ischemic attack, with duration and intensity balanced against bleeding risk.

evidence.reference_title: Benefits and Risks of Dual Versus Single Antiplatelet Therapy for Secondary Stroke Prevention: A Systematic Review for the 2021 Guideline for the Prevention of Stroke in Patients With Stroke and Transient Ischemic Attack.
evidence.snippet: DAPT was more effective than SAPT for prevention of secondary ischemic stroke when initiated early after the onset of minor stroke/high-risk transient ischemic attack and treatment duration was <90 days.

nodes.description: In situ thrombosis, artery-to-artery embolism, cardioembolism, or proximal large-vessel occlusion interrupts cerebral blood flow and initiates the ischemic cascade.

evidence.reference_title: The science of stroke: mechanisms in search of treatments.
evidence.snippet: This can develop either as a consequence of thrombosis in situ or following embolic occlusion of a cerebral blood vessel

nodes.description: Endovascular clot retrieval improves disability outcomes in selected patients with acute ischemic stroke caused by proximal anterior-circulation large-vessel occlusion.

evidence.reference_title: Endovascular thrombectomy after large-vessel ischaemic stroke: a meta-analysis of individual patient data from five randomised trials.
evidence.snippet: Endovascular thrombectomy led to significantly reduced disability at 90 days compared with control

nodes.description: Energy failure and impaired glutamate reuptake promote excitotoxic calcium influx, oxidative and nitrative stress, mitochondrial dysfunction, and apoptotic-like cell death in neurons and neighboring neurovascular cells.

evidence.reference_title: Cellular mechanisms of neurovascular damage and repair after stroke.
evidence.snippet: accumulated data over the past two decades have implicated excitotoxicity, oxidative stress and in some circumstances, apoptotic-like pathways
Brain injury following stroke results from a complex series of pathophysiological events including excitotoxicity, oxidative and nitrative stress, inflammation, and apoptosis.

nodes.description: Intravenous tissue plasminogen activator can restore perfusion in eligible early-presenting patients, improving 3-month functional outcome while increasing symptomatic intracerebral hemorrhage risk.

evidence.reference_title: Tissue plasminogen activator for acute ischemic stroke.
evidence.snippet: treatment with intravenous t-PA within three hours of the onset of ischemic stroke improved clinical outcome at three months.

nodes.description: Severe flow deficits rapidly deplete ATP in the ischemic core, while residual perfusion in the surrounding penumbra allows slower active cell death and creates a time-sensitive target for reperfusion therapy.

evidence.reference_title: Cellular mechanisms of neurovascular damage and repair after stroke.
evidence.snippet: In ischemic strokes, severe blood flow deficits in the core rapidly decrease ATP levels and energy stores.

evidence.reference_title: The science of stroke: mechanisms in search of treatments.
evidence.snippet: The ischemic penumbra then denotes an “at risk” region that is functionally impaired, but potentially salvageable.

nodes.description: Ischemic injury engages endothelial cells, astrocytes, neurons, microglia, and peripheral immune cells; inflammatory signaling, protease activation, and matrix injury can disrupt the blood-brain barrier and amplify tissue damage.

evidence.reference_title: The immunology of stroke: from mechanisms to translation.
evidence.snippet: Inflammatory signaling is involved in all stages of the ischemic cascade, from the early damaging events triggered by arterial occlusion

evidence.reference_title: Cellular mechanisms of neurovascular damage and repair after stroke.
evidence.snippet: The BBB homeostasis is remarkably dependent on endothelial-astrocyte-matrix interactions

nodes.description: Stroke prevention focuses on cardiovascular and brain-health domains including diet, activity, weight, sleep, glycemic control, blood pressure, lipids, and tobacco avoidance.
nodes.id: Primary prevention through vascular risk-factor control

evidence.reference_title: 2024 Guideline for the Primary Prevention of Stroke: A Guideline From the American Heart Association/American Stroke Association.
evidence.snippet: These recommendations align with the American Heart Association's Life's Essential 8 for optimizing cardiovascular and brain health
